verses.text: 'Blessed is he that considereth the poor,' for the LORD will deliver and sustain him in his own day of trouble. Mercy to the needy is richly repaid. God remembers the compassionate when they themselves are in need.

'Blessed is he that considereth the poor,' for the LORD will deliver and sustain him in his own day of trouble. Mercy to the needy is richly repaid. God remembers the compassionate when they themselves are in need.

'Blessed is he that considereth the poor,' for the LORD will deliver and sustain him in his own day of trouble. Mercy to the needy is richly repaid. God remembers the compassionate when they themselves are in need.

David prays, 'heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee,' while enemies wish him dead and speak vanity. He owns his sin amid their malice. The believer's first concern in trouble is to be right with God.

David prays, 'heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee,' while enemies wish him dead and speak vanity. He owns his sin amid their malice. The believer's first concern in trouble is to be right with God.

David prays, 'heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee,' while enemies wish him dead and speak vanity. He owns his sin amid their malice. The believer's first concern in trouble is to be right with God.

His foes whisper against him, and even 'mine own familiar friend... hath lifted up his heel against me.' This was fulfilled in Judas at the Last Supper. The betrayal of a trusted friend, felt by David, was deeply tasted by Christ.

His foes whisper against him, and even 'mine own familiar friend... hath lifted up his heel against me.' This was fulfilled in Judas at the Last Supper. The betrayal of a trusted friend, felt by David, was deeply tasted by Christ.

His foes whisper against him, and even 'mine own familiar friend... hath lifted up his heel against me.' This was fulfilled in Judas at the Last Supper. The betrayal of a trusted friend, felt by David, was deeply tasted by Christ.

He asks God to raise him up, confident that God upholds him in his integrity and sets him before His face for ever. Vindication is sought from God alone. The upheld believer stands not by his own strength but by God's favour.

He asks God to raise him up, confident that God upholds him in his integrity and sets him before His face for ever. Vindication is sought from God alone. The upheld believer stands not by his own strength but by God's favour.

He asks God to raise him up, confident that God upholds him in his integrity and sets him before His face for ever. Vindication is sought from God alone. The upheld believer stands not by his own strength but by God's favour.
'Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting.' The first book of Psalms closes in praise. However the psalm began, it ends blessing the eternal God.
